steps.title: Notice the target expression already appears
steps.content: The problem asks for the value of $2(x - 3)$, and this expression is already present in the given equation $44 = 6 + 2(x - 3)$. This means we can find the answer without solving for $x$ — we just need to isolate $2(x - 3)$.

steps.title: Isolate the target expression
steps.content:
Starting with:
$$44 = 6 + 2(x - 3)$$
Subtract 6 from both sides:
$$44 - 6 = 2(x - 3)$$
$$38 = 2(x - 3)$$
Therefore $2(x - 3) = 38$. That matches choice C — C is correct.
